meta.name: Pork Tamales (Red Chile)
meta.subtitle: Traditional masa tamales with a red chile pork filling, steamed in corn husks

summary: Classic pork tamales with a red chile filling and fluffy masa. This SSA baseline is written for deterministic planning: separate components (filling + masa + assembly + steam), batch scaling, and freezer-friendly storage.

yield.notes: Makes about 18–22 tamales depending on size and how thick you spread masa.

ingredients.label: Dried corn husks
ingredients.notes: Soak in hot water until pliable

ingredients.label: White onion
ingredients.notes: Half for pork cooking; half for sauce

ingredients.label: Garlic cloves
ingredients.notes: Some for pork cooking; some for sauce

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Divide across filling + masa; adjust to taste

ingredients.label: Masa harina
ingredients.notes: Fresh masa can be used; see variations

ingredients.label: Lard (or shortening)
ingredients.notes: For classic flavor; see dairy/veg substitutions in variations

ingredients.label: Warm broth/stock (chicken or pork)
ingredients.notes: Use reserved pork broth if available

steps.title: Soak corn husks
steps.body: Place corn husks in a large bowl or pot and cover with very hot water. Weigh down with a plate so they stay submerged. Soak until pliable, at least 30 minutes.

steps.title: Cook the pork until tender
steps.body: Add pork to a pot with half the onion (quartered), 3 cloves garlic, bay leaves (optional), and enough water to cover by 2 inches. Add ~1 tsp salt. Bring to a boil, then simmer gently until pork is very tender and shreds easily (75–100 minutes). Reserve broth.

steps.title: Toast and soak dried chiles
steps.body: Toast guajillo and ancho chiles briefly in a dry skillet until fragrant (10–20 seconds per side). Cover with hot water and soak 15 minutes until pliable.

steps.title: Blend red chile sauce
steps.body: Drain chiles (reserve a little soaking liquid). Blend chiles with remaining onion, remaining garlic, cumin/oregano (optional), vinegar (optional), and 1–2 cups reserved pork broth (or soak liquid) until very smooth. Optional: strain for extra-smooth sauce.

steps.title: Bloom chile sauce
steps.body: Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat. Carefully add chile sauce and cook 5–7 minutes, stirring, until darker and aromatic. Add chipotle in adobo if using. Season with salt to taste.
checks: Sauce tastes rich and not raw

steps.title: Shred pork and mix with sauce
steps.body: Remove pork from broth and shred. Combine shredded pork with enough red chile sauce to make a moist, spoonable filling. Add olives if using. Taste and adjust salt.

steps.title: Mix masa dough
steps.body: In a large bowl, whisk masa harina, baking powder, and 1 tsp salt. In a separate bowl (or stand mixer), beat lard until fluffy. Add dry masa mixture and warm broth gradually, mixing until a soft, spreadable dough forms. It should feel like thick peanut butter—spreadable but not runny.
checks: Masa spreads easily on a husk
Dough holds shape and doesn't crumble

steps.title: Assemble tamales
steps.body: Drain husks and pat dry. For each husk, spread about 2–3 tbsp masa into a rectangle (leave a border). Add 1–2 tbsp filling down the center. Fold sides in, then fold the bottom up. Tie with a strip of husk if desired. Repeat.
checks: Tamales are sealed enough to hold filling during steaming

steps.title: Steam tamales
steps.body: Stand tamales upright in a steamer basket (open end up). Add water to the pot below the basket (do not submerge tamales). Cover and steam 60–75 minutes, adding water as needed so the pot doesn't dry out. Tamales are done when the husk peels away cleanly from the masa.

steps.title: Rest and serve
steps.body: Let tamales rest 10 minutes before serving for best texture. Serve with salsa roja or salsa verde.

variations.label: Chicken Tamales
variations.notes: Swap pork for cooked shredded chicken thighs. Use chicken broth for the sauce and masa. Total cook time decreases.

variations.label: Tamales Verdes
variations.notes: Replace red chile sauce with salsa verde (tomatillo-based). Great with chicken or pork.

variations.label: Vegetarian Tamales
variations.notes: Use vegetable broth and a filling like roasted chile + cheese (or dairy-free cheese), beans, or sautéed mushrooms. Replace lard with vegetable shortening or whipped neutral oil-based fat.

variations.label: Pressure Cooker Pork
variations.notes: Cook pork with aromatics and water/broth for ~35 minutes high pressure + natural release. Proceed with shredding and sauce.

variations.label: Using Fresh Masa
variations.notes: If using fresh masa from a tortilleria, reduce added broth (start with ~1.5 cups) and adjust to spreadable consistency. Still whip fat for fluffiness.

storage.refrigeration: Refrigerate cooked tamales for up to 5 days.
storage.freezing: Freeze cooked tamales (wrapped) for up to 3 months. Best practice: freeze on a tray first, then bag.
storage.reheat: Steam frozen tamales 15–25 minutes until hot. Microwave works in a pinch: wrap in a damp paper towel and heat in short bursts.

notes: Do not let the steamer pot run dry; keep water below basket level.
Cool cooked tamales within 2 hours before refrigerating or freezing.

prepNotes: Make filling and sauce a day ahead for faster assembly day.
Soak husks first; keep a damp towel over husks while assembling to prevent drying.
planner.leftoversPlan: Freeze in 2–4 tamale packs for grab-and-go meals; include a salsa cup.

notes: Canonical SSA tamales baseline emphasizes component separation (filling/sauce/masa/assembly/steam) for planning and batch workflows.
Masa consistency is the main success factor: it must spread easily and steam to a set, tender texture.
For faster assembly, set up an assembly line: husks → spread masa → add filling → fold → stand in steamer.
